author: @yourekittenme.
text: I’m glad it worked for you; I haven’t had any luck using that trick.

author: @zattan2002
text: Nice trick. Also works with duck tape.

author: @bigsteve6729
text: Tape works too👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: A few people have mentioned this, I must definitely give it a go now! Thank you for the suggestion mate! :)

author: @theimpatientbrewer
text: Good tip Luke

author: @razzmancometh
text: A little friction always did calm my nerves after a particularly annoying disassembly job.. 😏

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Same here! until you're tightening it up then its goes, tight, tight, then righty loosy LOL. Thanks mate! :)

author: @paulbrouyere1735
text: Good tip, I ‘ll try it next time. But I think this will only work on not too large screws. ( Not the ones used for framing houses)

author: @schr4nz
text: My screw was too worn even for this, I used WD40, a hammer and a flat screwdriver to tap it out

author: @themeek351
text: Does it work on a Phillips screw?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I have used this trick on a Phillips before, It is definitely worth a go. Cheers mate! :)

author: @Twistedmetal-qe8kx
text: Thats cool, I'm going give that a try.  I get into lots of strippy situations too.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Same here! I always try this trick first lol. Thanks mate! :)

author: @PVflying
text: Thanks for that one Luke 👍👍 Have never seen this approach before, but I’ll try to remember it. 👌

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! It's my go-to trick if I have a stripped screw. :)

author: @3deeguy
text: 😀 Thanks.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: I will have to try it, up until now, I always used  a dab of valve grinding compound on the tip of my screwdriver.

author: @stevetobias4890
text: I love that hack also. There are a few tricks very similar to that all of which work on the same principle.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes! If I don't have a rubber band around, I would use a latex glove it's just a little harder to get the screwdriver to grip. It would be interesting to see what else you could use! Thank you mate! :)

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey electrical tape is another

author: @dr_ned_flanders
text: Noice

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Cheers mate! :)

author: @McRootbeer
text: I've never tried it before, but I hear you can get stripped screws out by using super glue to attach the bit to the screw.  To remove the screw from the bit you just burn the glue off with a lighter.  Anybody ever tried this?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: This definitely could work, it is always worth giving these things ago to see what works! This is just my go-to trick. :)

author: @randomscousebear869
text: Duct tape works a treat too 😁👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome, I must give that a go! Thanks mate! :)

author: @richardgregory4640
text: "Revoming"! Maybe try spellchecker for your title shot!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Whoops! Should be sorted now. Thank you mate! :)

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: I’ve seen quite a few ways. I’ve never seen that.

author: @colleenforrest7936
text: Nice!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey

author: @oildalestar
text: I have heard that this doesn't work every time

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: It's true, This is always my go trick I would say it works 9/10 times but it depends on the screw I think. If it doesn't work for me I bring out the big guns and drill the pain out LOL. Thanks mate! :)

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text: Thank you for this, my Dad taught me this one, whilst I was a mere slip of a girl, thank you so much for reminding me, and, letting me wander down memory lane for a while!!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is awesome! I have no idea where I picked this up from but it's an awesome little trick, but it does the job. Thank you! :)

author: @hempev
text: I have several anticlockwise bits for doing this, but a rubber band is a slick trick!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
